lenses.guidance: vv 1-49's forty-two stages — 'they set out from X and camped at Y' — repeat one verbal formula across nearly fifty verses. The structure refuses summary; each leg is named. Repetition itself becomes the chapter's testimony to providence.

vv 50-56's command to drive out the inhabitants and destroy idols closes the wilderness account by anchoring the redemptive arc forward into Joshua. The promise begun with Abraham reaches the threshold; covenant faithfulness expects covenant action.
